title: Pan-Seared Bass with Mediterranean Flair
description: A Chinese-inspired bass dish with a Mediterranean twist, featuring the rich flavors of the sea and a hint of indulgence.

ingredients.name: Indulgent Honey
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality honey for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Mediterranean Oregano
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried oregano works well in this recipe.

ingredients.name: Sea Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Mediterranean Lemons
ingredients.notes: Use fresh lemons for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality, salted butter for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before sautéing for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Green Onions
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice the green onions before using.

instructions: Season the bass fillets with sea salt and Mediterranean oregano.
Heat the butter in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the bass fillets for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until the bass is cooked through.
While the bass is cooking, mix the honey, garlic, and green onions in a small bowl.
After the bass is done cooking, remove it from the skillet and brush the honey mixture on top of each fillet.
Return the skillet to the stovetop and sauté the Mediterranean olives for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Serve the bass fillets with the sautéed olives and a squeeze of Mediterranean lemon juice.
Garnish with additional green onions and a sprinkle of sea salt, if desired.
